steps.title: Identify what the evidence demonstrates
steps.content: The passage shows that “life” is the ninth most commonly used noun in a corpus. This evidence would support a hypothesis that “life” is something very frequent and widespread in written English.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Ubiquitous means present everywhere and very commonly found — exactly what the evidence demonstrates. The other three contradict or miss the point: “authoritative” relates to credibility rather than frequency, “diminishing” contradicts the high frequency shown, and “misused” describes incorrect usage rather than how common a word is. This matches choice A — A is correct.
